title: 2026 U.S. Senate election in Montana
description: Montana voters will elect one U.S. senator in 2026 for the state’s Class II seat. The race matters because it will help determine control of the U.S. Senate, and Ballotpedia rates the contest as leaning strongly Republican with the seat currently held by Steve Daines. The field includes a crowded set of primary candidates across parties, with Republicans emphasizing continuity and Democrats highlighting affordability, health care, and public services.

candidates.name: Steve Daines
candidates.summary: Steve Daines is the sitting U.S. senator from Montana and a former businessman. He entered the 2026 cycle as the incumbent in the race for Montana’s Class II Senate seat, but Ballotpedia notes he withdrew from the Republican primary filing process, so his status in the race should be treated cautiously. His public profile and campaign messaging have emphasized conservative priorities such as jobs, freedom, border security, and Montana values.

summary_sources.title: United States Senate election in Montana, 2026 - Ballotpedia
Steve Daines for Montana | Faith, Freedom & Opportunity
Steve Daines - Ballotpedia

candidates.name: Reilly Neill
candidates.summary: Reilly Neill is a Democratic candidate for U.S. Senate in Montana and a former state legislator. Her campaign describes her as a different kind of Democrat focused on affordability, public education, agriculture, health care, and listening to voters across the state. She has framed her campaign as a grassroots effort aimed at challenging the status quo and restoring trust in leadership.

candidates.name: Kurt Alme
candidates.summary: Kurt Alme is a Republican candidate for U.S. Senate in Montana and previously served as the U.S. attorney for the District of Montana. His background is in law and federal prosecution, and his entry into the race came late in the filing period. Public reporting and Ballotpedia identify him as one of the Republican primary contenders.

candidates.name: Lee Calhoun
candidates.summary: Lee Calhoun is a Republican candidate in Montana’s 2026 U.S. Senate primary. Ballotpedia lists him among the Republican contenders, and campaign materials describe him as a Montana conservative focused on economic opportunity, health care, and community issues. Public reporting indicates he later suspended or withdrew his bid, so his active status should be checked against the latest filing records.

summary_sources.title: Lee Calhoun - Ballotpedia
Charles Walking Child for US Senate
BREAKING: Republican Candidate Lee Calhoun Withdraws from Montana Senate Race

candidates.name: Charles A. Walking Child
candidates.summary: Charles A. Walking Child is a Republican candidate in Montana’s 2026 U.S. Senate primary. His campaign says he is focused on economic opportunity, accessible health care, and conservative values, and Ballotpedia lists him among the Republican contenders. Public biographical material describes him as a Montana native with Blackfeet heritage and a background shaped by both hardship and faith.

summary_sources.title: Charles A. Walking Child - Ballotpedia
Charles Walking Child for US Senate
Charles Walkingchild | U.S. Senate | 2024 Montana Election Guide

candidates.name: Seth Bodnar
candidates.summary: Seth Bodnar is an independent candidate for U.S. Senate in Montana and previously served as president of the University of Montana. His background combines higher education leadership, military service, and business experience, and he entered the race as a late-filed general-election candidate. Ballotpedia lists him as the current general-election candidate, making him the main independent option in the race.

summary_sources.title: United States Senate election in Montana, 2026 - Ballotpedia

candidates.name: Michael Black Wolf
candidates.summary: Michael Black Wolf is listed by Ballotpedia as a Democratic primary candidate for Montana’s 2026 U.S. Senate race. Publicly available biographical detail is limited in the sources reviewed, so his background and issue profile are not well documented in the materials found. He remains one of several Democrats competing for the nomination.

candidates.name: Michael Hummert
candidates.summary: Michael Hummert is a Democratic candidate in Montana’s 2026 U.S. Senate primary and previously ran in the 2024 Democratic primary for the same seat. Ballotpedia lists him among the current Democratic contenders, but the sources reviewed did not provide much additional biographical detail. He is part of a crowded Democratic field that is competing to challenge the Republican nominee in November.
